title: The One Where Rachel Goes Back To Work

jokes.text: You're gonna get me good and pregnant.
jokes.setup: Monica making breakfast for Chandler with romantic intentions
jokes.punchline: Overly clinical/crude way of describing baby-making
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica character moment - her hyper-organized nature applied to procreation in the most unsexy way possible. Great economy and character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I made you breakfast. I was thinking we could go back to bed...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh wow, that sounds great! So what do you want to do, practice making a baby?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, yes...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Because I gotta tell you, I've been reading about this. Did you know that the average ejaculation contains 200 million sperm?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: And only one of them is gonna make it. So really, what we're doing here is sending 200 million soldiers out to war, and only one comes home.

jokes.text: Well, I just lost my erection.
jokes.setup: Chandler's unemployment anxiety hitting during Monica's romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Physical manifestation of stress interrupting baby-making plans
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler deadpan delivery. Perfect economy and timing, though the concept isn't groundbreaking.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler, we've been trying for so long. Tonight's the night. I can feel it.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I know, I know. I'm ready. I'm totally ready.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Then what's wrong?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Nothing! Nothing's wrong. I'm just... I'm a little nervous about the job situation, that's all.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler, not tonight, please.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You're right, you're right. I'm sorry.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh God, what if I can't... what if I'm not man enough to provide for a family? What if—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could I BE any more unemployed?

jokes.text: Hey, this is where your hyper-organized pain-in-the-ass stuff pays off.
jokes.setup: Monica offering to organize Chandler's job search
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about Monica's controlling nature being useful
jokes.explanation: Good character work recognizing Monica's personality traits, delivered in Chandler's typical style.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I could help you organize your job search. You know how I love organizing things.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, that's true. And you know what? For once, your obsessive need to control everything might actually be useful.

jokes.text: My erection is back!
jokes.setup: Monica's organization skills making Chandler feel better about unemployment
jokes.punchline: Physical arousal returning when life feels manageable
jokes.explanation: Solid callback structure but predictable. Works well as a topper to the earlier joke.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You know, Monica, I have to say... watching you organize everything so perfectly, it's actually making me feel better about not having a job.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Really? That's great!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, I mean, at least one of us has our life together. And honestly, for the first time in weeks, I'm starting to feel... I don't know... a little bit aroused?

jokes.text: Thank you. Phoebe, have you ever been bitten by a hungry Italian?
jokes.setup: Phoebe taking Joey's coffee and muffin
jokes.punchline: Joey's stereotypical Italian heritage mixed with hunger creating mock-threat
jokes.explanation: Plays into Joey's food obsession and Italian background. Clever wording but relies on ethnic stereotype.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey! That's my coffee and my muffin!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I know, I'm sorry, I'm just really hungry.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Phoebe, you don't understand. In my family, you don't take a man's food. You just don't do it.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: My grandfather once threw a guy out a window over a cannoli.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, but he would have if he'd had the energy. So I'm gonna give you five seconds to put that muffin down.

jokes.text: That's why Richard Dreyfuss and I don't speak anymore.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining why she doesn't borrow money from friends
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of friendship with famous actor as if it's normal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe absurdism - random celebrity connection delivered matter-of-factly. High originality and character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I don't borrow money from friends because it ruins the relationship. Like, I lent Brad Pitt twenty bucks once and we haven't spoken in years.

jokes.text: Yeah, right, the unemployed piano player who gave his trust fund to charity? I'm splitting the muffin with him.
jokes.setup: Suggestion that Phoebe could get money from her boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Two-part reveal of boyfriend's noble poverty and resulting food sharing
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure showing the depth of their poverty. Character-consistent for Phoebe's dating choices.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Why don't you just ask your boyfriend for money?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, he doesn't have any. He's really noble and poor.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, how do you two survive?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: We share. He eats what I eat.

jokes.text: Really? No, it is awesome.
jokes.setup: Joey warning Phoebe that TV isn't glamorous
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal revealing he was just being modest
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection with perfect economy. Simple but effective structure, very Joey-like.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You know, Phoebe, TV isn't as glamorous as it seems. It's hard work, long hours, a lot of rejection...

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh wow, that sounds really tough.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, but then you get one good role and suddenly you're on a billboard in Times Square, beautiful women are throwing themselves at you, and you're making more money than you know what to do with.

jokes.text: Because if one more person says, 'What a cute little boy'... I'm gonna whip them with a car antenna.
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining why Emma has a pink bow taped to her head
jokes.punchline: Extreme violent threat over gender misidentification
jokes.explanation: Specific, unexpected weapon choice elevates standard parental frustration joke. Good escalation to violence.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, she kept getting mistaken for a boy, so I put a bow on her head.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, so if someone mistakes her for a boy again, what are you gonna do? Kill them?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yes.

jokes.text: Yeah, she's gonna be hotter than peasant blouses and A-line skirts. Can I get a blue bow?
jokes.setup: Discussion of Emma being popular at Ralph Lauren office
jokes.punchline: Ross wanting fashion accessory like a baby, mixing fashion terms awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Ross being awkward around fashion terminology, wanting to fit in with baby's accessories. Mild character comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: A baby! I want a baby! You know, something I can carry around, you know, show it off...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's called a baby, Ross.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, no, no! I want one that's like a fashion accessory. You know, something that says 'Look at me, I'm a man with a baby!'

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Isn't that just a baby?

jokes.text: You call eight hours alone with my label maker wasted time? Now I'll get to use my shredder!
jokes.setup: Chandler apologizing for wasting Monica's research time
jokes.punchline: Monica's joy at using office supplies reveals her true motivation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica characterization - her obsession with organization tools is the real reward. Excellent character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, I'm sorry I wasted all your research time.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, don't worry about it! Besides, I got to use the office supplies!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, that's why you helped me?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well... I mean... they have those really good pens!

jokes.text: Cheese. It's milk that you chew.
jokes.setup: Chandler demonstrating his slogan-writing skills
jokes.punchline: Overly literal, childlike description of cheese
jokes.explanation: Brilliant anti-slogan that's memorable for being so wrong. Perfect economy and high quotability factor.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could the cheese BE any more yellow?

jokes.text: Crackers. Because your cheese needs a buddy.
jokes.setup: Continuing slogan demonstration
jokes.punchline: Anthropomorphizing food with friendship needs
jokes.explanation: Continues the anti-slogan theme with childlike logic. Good follow-up but not as strong as the cheese line.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could that BE any more of a friendship slogan? 'Friends don't let friends eat stale bread.' See, the bread is lonely. It needs friends.

jokes.text: A grape. Because who can get a watermelon in your mouth?
jokes.setup: Third slogan attempt
jokes.punchline: Bizarre comparative logic about fruit size
jokes.explanation: Weakest of the slogan trilogy. The logic is more confusing than funny, losing the simple charm of the earlier ones.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could the slogan be any more fruit-based?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What's wrong with it?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, it compares apples to oranges. Literally. And then it says they're the same size, which they're not.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Actually, depending on the variety—

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could you be any more literal right now?

jokes.text: Socks. Because your family's feet deserve the best.
jokes.setup: Monica trying her hand at slogans
jokes.punchline: Actually competent slogan that highlights Chandler's incompetence
jokes.explanation: The joke is that Monica is actually good at this, contrasting with Chandler's absurd attempts. Character-appropriate but not particularly funny.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What about: 'Chandler Bing: He Tries'?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's... actually not bad.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Right? It's honest, it's humble, it really captures your essence.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, what essence? 'He Tries'? That's the bar? That's what we're celebrating now?

jokes.text: The phone. Bringing you closer to people who have phones.
jokes.setup: Chandler continuing to demonstrate his slogan skills
jokes.punchline: Circular logic that explains nothing
jokes.explanation: Returns to the strong anti-slogan form with tautological logic. Good economy and absurdist appeal.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: The key to a great slogan is circular logic that explains nothing. Like, 'It is what it is.'

jokes.text: Marriage. It's not for everybody.
jokes.setup: Final slogan attempt
jokes.punchline: Anti-marriage message from newlywed, terrible advertising advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and ironic given Chandler's recent marriage. Shows his anxiety about commitment even in fake slogans.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could we BE any more married?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler, that's not a good slogan for a wedding company.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Why not? It's catchy!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Because it sounds like you're warning people AGAINST getting married!

jokes.text: Yeah, after you punched that one guy who got it wrong, word spread.
jokes.setup: Rachel saying people knew Emma was a girl
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Rachel actually hit someone over gender confusion
jokes.explanation: Pays off the earlier threat by showing Rachel actually acted on it. Solid callback structure.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Everyone knew Emma was a girl!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What are you talking about? Of course they knew!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, that guy at the coffee shop kept saying 'he' and I hit him!

jokes.text: Who the hell are you? Who the hell are you? I'm the hell person whose office this is. Good one, Rach.
jokes.setup: Rachel and Gavin having territorial confrontation
jokes.punchline: Rachel's attempt at clever wordplay falling flat, Gavin's sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Rachel's failed attempt at wit followed by Gavin's deadpan response. The humor is in Rachel's failure rather than success.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You know what? This is MY office space. I'm not going to be pushed around by some guy named Gavin.

dialogue.speaker: Gavin
dialogue.line: Oh wow, a 'guy named Gavin' joke. That's... really cutting edge, Rachel.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, maybe if you weren't always invading my personal space, I'd have time to come up with better material.

dialogue.speaker: Gavin
dialogue.line: Right, because that's definitely the problem here.

jokes.text: Your baby's so cute. But why would you put a pink bow on a boy?
jokes.setup: Earlier setup about gender confusion and pink bow solution
jokes.punchline: New character immediately making the mistake the bow was meant to prevent
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing the futility of Rachel's solution. Good ironic structure but predictable payoff.

dialogue.speaker: Gavin
dialogue.line: Oh, what a beautiful baby girl!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Actually, it's a boy.

dialogue.speaker: Gavin
dialogue.line: Oh... well, the pink bow threw me off.

jokes.text: Sorry, I'm just so used to hitting on the extras.
jokes.setup: Joey making suggestive comment to Phoebe in nurse costume
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of inappropriate workplace behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey womanizing behavior, delivered matter-of-factly. Good character work but relies on established traits.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh wow, a nurse! You know, I have a very strict policy about dating girls in uniforms.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh really?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, I don't.

jokes.text: All right, eight.
jokes.setup: Joey saying it usually takes him three takes, then Phoebe messing up again
jokes.punchline: Revealing it actually takes Joey many more attempts than he claimed
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Joey's earlier claim was false modesty. Economy and timing work well.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: It usually takes me three takes.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh no, I messed up again!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, well... actually it takes me like twenty takes. Sometimes thirty. Last week I did fifty-two takes of a scene where I just had to say 'hello.'

jokes.text: Bagels and doughnuts. Round food for every mood.
jokes.setup: Chandler demonstrating his slogan skills to ad executive
jokes.punchline: Another terrible slogan focusing on irrelevant shape similarity
jokes.explanation: Continues the bad slogan running gag. Less inspired than the earlier examples but maintains consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: How about this: 'A donut within a donut.' It's a donut... shaped like a donut.

jokes.text: That's funny. When you said 'unpaid,' it sounded like you said 'unpaid.'
jokes.setup: Ad executive offering unpaid internship
jokes.punchline: Chandler's disbelief expressed through repetition
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm dealing with disappointment. Good structure but familiar character response.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: An unpaid internship? Could I BE any more uninterested?

jokes.text: Well, I can skip a couple of steps, right? I mean, you did hear my round food thing.
jokes.setup: Being told he needs to start at bottom and build portfolio
jokes.punchline: Chandler's delusional confidence in his terrible slogan
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Chandler's misplaced pride in his awful work. Self-awareness makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could I BE any more ready to start at the bottom?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I've come up with a slogan for the company: 'We're number two, but we try harder!'

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? It's got a nice ring to it!

jokes.text: I tend to keep talking till somebody stops me.
jokes.setup: Chandler oversharing about Monica being naked at home
jokes.punchline: Self-aware comment about his own tendency to overshare
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about Chandler's character trait. Self-awareness elevates what could be a simple awkward moment.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: She walks around naked all the time. I mean, it's not a big deal. She's very comfortable with her body. Sometimes I'll just be sitting on the couch and—why am I telling you this?

jokes.text: A vacation? My idea of a vacation does not involve... something sucking on my nipples until they are raw.
jokes.setup: Gavin calling maternity leave a 'baby vacation'
jokes.punchline: Graphic description of breastfeeding reality
jokes.explanation: Raw honesty about motherhood challenges. Gets laughs from uncomfortable truth rather than clever wordplay.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: A baby vacation? Do you know what it's actually like? You're leaking from places you didn't know could leak, you haven't slept in 72 hours, and your body feels like it's been hit by a truck. That's not a vacation, that's a hostage situation!

jokes.text: Clearly, you've never been to Sandals Paradise Island.
jokes.setup: Rachel's graphic description of breastfeeding pain
jokes.punchline: Implication that the resort offers similar painful experiences
jokes.explanation: Unexpected dark turn making light of resort experiences. Good economy and surprising twist.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: My nipples are so sore, I can barely stand to have anything touch them.

dialogue.speaker: Gavin
dialogue.line: Well, you should try our spa. We have a great exfoliating treatment.

jokes.text: Rach, we have a code brown situation.
jokes.setup: Rachel in tense office confrontation
jokes.punchline: Ross interrupting with euphemism for dirty diaper
jokes.explanation: Standard bathroom humor with military/hospital terminology. Functional but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I need to talk to you about the report you filed.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh, um, well actually there's been a code brown in the nursery.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What?

jokes.text: And what department was that? The jerk department?
jokes.setup: Gavin explaining he was transferred from another department
jokes.punchline: Rachel's childish insult
jokes.explanation: Weak elementary school-level insult. Shows Rachel's frustration but lacks wit or originality.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yeah, well you're a big doody head!

jokes.text: Well, did they mention that I'm rubber and you're glue?
jokes.setup: Gavin mentioning they told him about Rachel's quick wit
jokes.punchline: Rachel proving the opposite with playground comeback
jokes.explanation: Self-defeating humor - Rachel's 'wit' being incredibly juvenile. The irony works but the base joke is weak.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? Well... you're a... you're a poopy head!

jokes.text: Super ass-kissing power.
jokes.setup: Gavin being called 'Super Gavin' by their boss
jokes.punchline: Rachel identifying his real superpower as sycophancy
jokes.explanation: Plays off the superhero theme but with a common workplace observation. Decent callback structure.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, so your boss actually calls you 'Super Gavin'?

dialogue.speaker: Gavin
dialogue.line: Yeah, because I'm always going the extra mile.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, I think his real superpower is sycophancy.

jokes.text: See, for a superhero, not so much with the listening.
jokes.setup: Gavin asking when she's coming back after she said two weeks
jokes.punchline: Continuing superhero theme to mock his inattention
jokes.explanation: Maintains the superhero running gag but without much additional humor. Functional callback.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Two weeks.

dialogue.speaker: Gavin
dialogue.line: Two weeks? But you said you'd be back in two weeks!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I know, that's what I just said.

dialogue.speaker: Gavin
dialogue.line: Right, right. So... two weeks from now?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, Gavin. Two weeks from when I left. Which was two weeks ago. So I'll be back... now. I'm back now. I'm standing right here.

jokes.text: I ran out of diapers, and I had to use your sweater. I didn't bring a sweater. Then, we should really go.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining they need to leave quickly
jokes.punchline: Revelation that he used someone else's clothing as a diaper
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure with Ross's panic revealing worse and worse details. Physical comedy potential.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: We need to leave right now!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What? Why? What happened?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I ran out of diapers for the baby and I may have... used Chandler's pants.

jokes.text: So he said, 'If your puppy friend doesn't get her puppy act together... I am gonna fire her mother-puppy ass.'
jokes.setup: Joey censoring the director's harsh words by replacing profanity with 'puppy'
jokes.punchline: The absurd contrast between cute word and threatening message
jokes.explanation: Creative censorship creates absurd juxtaposition. Good character work showing Joey's protective nature toward Phoebe.

dialogue.speaker: Director
dialogue.line: You're a talentless, no-good puppy!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, that's better!

jokes.text: She's Nurse With Tray. Nurse With Tray doesn't know Joey. She has no time for friends. She gets in that operating room and she carries that tray to the doctor... because if she doesn't, people die. Who dies? Man With Eye Patch!
jokes.setup: Phoebe saying she can't act
jokes.punchline: Joey's intense Method acting approach to being an extra with a tray
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Joey's acting pretensions applied to the most mundane role. Perfect character integration and absurdist escalation.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I can't act.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What are you talking about? Of course you can act.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No, I really can't. I have no idea what I'm doing.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Pheebs, I'm gonna teach you. You see, it's all about the Method. You gotta become the character. Like, I'm an extra on this movie, right? I'm carrying a tray. But I'm not just carrying a tray—I'm a man who's been carrying this tray his whole life. This tray is my identity. This tray is my legacy.

jokes.text: Hey, you know what I was thinking? I don't know if 'code brown' is the best code word. I think people might know what we're talking about.
jokes.setup: Earlier use of 'code brown' euphemism
jokes.punchline: Ross's delayed realization that the euphemism is too obvious
jokes.explanation: Mild callback that acknowledges the weakness of the earlier joke. Ross-like overthinking but not particularly funny.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Wait... 'code brown'? That's... that's not even a euphemism, that's just describing it!

jokes.text: All right, calm down, Norma Rae. They didn't actually say that.
jokes.setup: Ross getting angry about illegal firing threat
jokes.punchline: Rachel comparing Ross to famous labor activist, then admitting she exaggerated
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference with Ross's tendency toward righteous anger. The admission deflates the moment effectively.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Ross, you're like César Chávez!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, okay, I don't really know who César Chávez is, but you're fighting for what's right!

jokes.text: Absolutely. Just give me your breasts, and we'll be on our way.
jokes.setup: Ross agreeing to take care of Emma for the day
jokes.punchline: Awkward phrasing about breastfeeding needs
jokes.explanation: Ross's scientific/awkward approach to practical parenting needs. Mild discomfort humor from poor word choice.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I can totally take care of Emma for the day.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Are you sure? She needs to be breastfed.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I know, I know. I'll just... I'll feed her the breast milk you pump.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, but you have to be really careful with how you handle it.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? I've handled breasts before.

jokes.text: Ross. You're pretty.
jokes.setup: Ross making faces at baby Emma
jokes.punchline: Ross's reaction to baby apparently complimenting him
jokes.explanation: Ross's vanity and need for validation extending to babies. Simple but character-appropriate moment.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh, you like that? You like Daddy making faces?

dialogue.speaker: Emma
dialogue.line: Goo goo ga ga!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well thank you! That's very sweet of you to say.

jokes.text: Yeah. Goodbye.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking if Chandler has to tell Monica he doesn't want a baby now
jokes.punchline: Chandler's fatalistic acceptance and immediate exit
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character work. Chandler's dramatic tendencies shown through extreme brevity and resignation.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, I'm gonna die alone. Could be today, could be 60 years from now. Either way, I should probably get out of here.

jokes.text: Didn't see you there, Geller.
jokes.setup: Monica ready for baby-making, Ross unexpectedly present
jokes.punchline: Monica's awkward acknowledgment while in compromising position
jokes.explanation: Awkward family dynamics with sexual timing. Visual comedy implied from Monica's state of undress.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh! Ross! Hi! I didn't know you were here.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, I just came by to—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We were just... this isn't what it looks like.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Actually, it's exactly what it looks like.

jokes.text: Let me put it this way: We're having sex whether you're here or not. Pants off, Bing.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to delay their baby-making plans
jokes.punchline: Monica's determination overriding social norms
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica determination and control issues. Uncomfortable but character-driven humor with good escalation.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Ross, we've been talking about this for months. I'm ready to start trying for a baby.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I know, I know, but maybe we should wait until next year? You know, let's enjoy being married first.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Ross, we got married eight months ago. My biological clock is ticking louder than a time bomb in a sitcom.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Can't we just... ease into it?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Ease into it? I'm ovulating RIGHT NOW, and I will drag you into that bedroom if I have to.

jokes.text: Hey, they were popular when I left.
jokes.setup: Gavin mentioning he changed Rachel's NSYNC screensaver
jokes.punchline: Rachel's defensive justification for outdated boy band preference
jokes.explanation: Mild generational/cultural humor about changing pop culture. Rachel's embarrassment is relatable but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What? NSYNC is timeless! They're like the Beatles of the '90s.

jokes.text: Of course, by that time, ties will be obsolete... and we'll all be wearing silver jumpsuits.
jokes.setup: Ross saying Chandler has to return the tie when he gets a job
jokes.punchline: Ross's pessimistic future prediction about fashion and Chandler's employment
jokes.explanation: Ross's tendency toward dramatic pessimism combined with sci-fi fashion prediction. Character-appropriate but mild humor.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, when Chandler gets a job, he can return it.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: By then, ties will probably be out of fashion anyway.

jokes.text: What? You faked it? You know what? I don't need a tie.
jokes.setup: Chandler admitting he faked an orgasm
jokes.punchline: Ross's traumatic reaction and immediate exit
jokes.explanation: Ross's awkward reaction to overhearing intimate details about his sister. Good character work and physical comedy timing.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You faked it?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh my God. Oh my God!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I can't... I can't be here right now.

jokes.text: Well, it's not okay, because I gave a very memorable performance as the nurse. And now, suddenly, I'm the waitress? That's gonna confuse my fans.
jokes.setup: Phoebe objecting to playing different background roles
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's delusion about having fans from her extra work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe delusion and self-importance about minor background work. Good escalation from professional concern to ego.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I can't play the same character twice. What if one of my fans saw me playing two different people? They'd think I'm a sellout.

jokes.text: Sometimes we use the same extras for different parts. It's okay. Well, maybe you are a nurse... but you moonlight as a waitress. Because I'm a single mother supporting my two children.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's objection to playing multiple roles
jokes.punchline: Joey and Phoebe building elaborate backstory for extra character
jokes.explanation: Good escalation as they create unnecessary complexity for background roles. Shows both characters' tendency toward overcomplication.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I don't want to play multiple roles.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Come on, Phoebe. It's just one extra character.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Fine. But he has to have a complete backstory.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, okay. He's a... he's a dentist from Portland.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: A dentist? Why would he be a dentist? What made him want to be a dentist?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Um... his father was a dentist?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No, no, no. His father was a florist. And his mother left when he was young, so he became a dentist to fill the void.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: That's... actually really good. And he has a dog.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: A golden retriever named Buttons, who died in 1987.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Phoebe, that's so dark.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: That's why he became a dentist.

jokes.text: No. No, see, you and Drake are having a fight. About what? He slept with you... Best sex you ever had, by the way. And then never called you. And I just wanted a new daddy for Davey and Becky.
jokes.setup: Building backstory for why Phoebe's characters would ignore Joey's character
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate soap opera drama with Joey's ego insertion
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure building soap opera complexity. Joey's ego and Phoebe's creativity combining well.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So why does your character hate my character?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, I killed your father.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, but then it turned out he was actually my real father too.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait... so you're my sister?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Half-sister. And then I slept with you.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Pheebs!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: But I didn't know at the time. And it was really, really good.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, okay... so that's why she ignores me.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: And then I had your baby, but the baby turned out to be the reincarnation of her dead mother.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: The baby was her mother?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, and the baby didn't like you either.

jokes.text: What are you doing? I'm very angry at him because he slept with me and never called me back. Me too. Me too!
jokes.setup: Phoebe acting out her backstory during the scene
jokes.punchline: Other extras getting caught up in the fictional drama
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the backstory setup with escalation as it spreads to other extras. Physical comedy implied.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: So then my mother ran off with a circus clown, and my father went to prison for a crime he didn't commit!

dialogue.speaker: Extra 1
dialogue.line: Oh my God, that's terrible!

dialogue.speaker: Extra 2
dialogue.line: Your poor mother!

dialogue.speaker: Director
dialogue.line: Cut! That's great, everyone. Let's move on.

dialogue.speaker: Extra 1
dialogue.line: Wait, what happened to her father? Did he escape?

dialogue.speaker: Extra 2
dialogue.line: Yeah, and did she ever find her mother?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Uh, guys, it's just a scene.

dialogue.speaker: Extra 1
dialogue.line: Just a scene?! That woman's life is a tragedy!

jokes.text: But I love that man. I'd follow him into hell. Microphones.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they need to do presentation now because Ralph is helicopter shopping
jokes.punchline: Bizarre declaration of devotion followed by random word
jokes.explanation: Bizarre corporate devotion combined with random word suggests character's mental state. Absurdist but inconsistent.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Zelner
dialogue.line: We need to do the presentation now because Ralph is helicopter shopping.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Zelner
dialogue.line: I love him more than my own life, and I will follow him to the ends of the earth!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Zelner
dialogue.line: Giddy-up!

jokes.text: No one will remember that I worked here, and then Ralph will buy his helicopter... and Super Gavin will just fly right alongside of him.
jokes.setup: Rachel's frustration about Gavin taking over
jokes.punchline: Elaborate fantasy of Gavin literally flying with superhero powers
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the superhero metaphor to absurd literal level. Shows Rachel's emotional state through exaggeration.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: He just swoops in here with his stupid cape and his stupid powers, and suddenly he's running the whole department!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I can see it now — Gavin Man, flying around the office, dodging bullets, catching falling staplers with his bare hands...

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Meanwhile I'm just sitting at my desk like some kind of idiot, watching him hover over my head while I'm trying to work!

jokes.text: Because you worked really hard, and it's your job. And you're a little crazy.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking why Gavin would help her
jokes.punchline: Gavin's honest assessment including Rachel's mental state
jokes.explanation: Gavin's blunt honesty tempered with genuine concern. Character development moment with mild humor.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Why would you help me?

dialogue.speaker: Gavin
dialogue.line: Because you're clearly unstable and I'm a little worried about you.

jokes.text: Listen, Phoebe. I was just talking to the director... How about I do something for you? Tomorrow, I'll bring you a hat, cover up the bald.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to help Phoebe with the director
jokes.punchline: Joey insulting the director's appearance while trying to make a deal
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey obliviousness making situation worse while trying to help. Good cringe factor and character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Look, I'm just saying, you gotta give Phoebe a shot. She's got real talent.

dialogue.speaker: Director
dialogue.line: I don't know, Joey. I'm not convinced.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh come on! What's it gonna take? And don't say 'a face that doesn't look like a raw steak.'

jokes.text: What? I'm so good in the scene that I'm stealing focus from you. Rise to the challenge, Tribbiani, because I just raised the bar. Come join me up here.
jokes.setup: Joey saying Phoebe is threatened by his performance
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's grandiose response about her extra work stealing focus
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's delusion escalating to dramatic acting competition language. Good character work with theatrical pretension.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Threatened? I'm not threatened! I do extra work so people will look at me instead of you. I have to do a lot of extra work.

jokes.text: Yeah, you can fire her, but I would call security. She won't go easy.
jokes.setup: Director considering firing Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Joey's warning about Phoebe's potential violent reaction
jokes.explanation: Joey's protective but realistic assessment of Phoebe's temperament. Character-driven with mild escalation.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! You can't fire Phoebe!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Do you have any idea what she's capable of? She wrote a song called 'Smelly Cat.' Do you really want to make an enemy out of someone like that?

jokes.text: Yes, you can. You just make the faces and the noises. Guys can fake it? Unbelievable. The one thing that's ours.
jokes.setup: Monica disbelieving that men can fake orgasms
jokes.punchline: Chandler's matter-of-fact explanation and Monica's territorial response
jokes.explanation: Plays on gender stereotypes about sexual performance with Monica's competitive nature. Decent structure and character work.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, sure. It's not that hard. You just go like this: Oh! Oh! Oh!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It's all in the inflection.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, no, no. You're not doing that in my apartment.

jokes.text: Pants. Like shorts but longer.
jokes.setup: Chandler explaining why his career transition might take time
jokes.punchline: Another terrible slogan demonstrating his lack of talent
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and absurdist logic. One of the best examples of the bad slogan running gag with maximum quotability.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could I BE any worse at coming up with slogans?

jokes.text: What if the baby gets into the ribbon drawer... and messes up all the ribbons? What if there's no room for a ribbon drawer... because the baby's stuff takes up all the space? Where will all the ribbons go?
jokes.setup: Monica worrying about losing control with a baby
jokes.punchline: Her concerns escalating to specific organizational obsessions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica character work with escalating organizational anxiety. Excellent structure building to absurd specificity about ribbons.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What if I can't handle a baby? What if I lose control?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, you're going to be a great mother.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You don't understand! What if the baby's socks don't match? What if the diaper bag isn't color-coordinated? What if—oh God—what if there are wrinkles in the crib sheets?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, babies don't care about—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I need to alphabetize the baby formula. By expiration date AND brand name. And the pacifiers need to be arranged by size in a labeled drawer with dividers!

jokes.text: I know you botched that operation on purpose. I can't prove it yet, but when I do, you'll be going to jail for murder. I don't care if you are my brother. I'm not your brother. What about my children, Drake? No! No! No!
jokes.setup: Phoebe finally performing her scene as the waitress
jokes.punchline: Unleashing all the elaborate backstory drama in inappropriate context
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the entire Phoebe subplot with perfect escalation. All the built-up drama exploding in the wrong context.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, um, would you like to start with some coffee? My father was a dairy farmer, but he lost everything in a tornado. My mother ran off with a circus clown. I've been raising my seven siblings alone while working three jobs.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We just want coffee.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I haven't slept in six years. I have a skin condition. And sometimes I see dead people.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Phoebe, it's just coffee!

tmdb_overview: Chandler starts looking for a job and is anxious about having a baby with Monica while uncertain about his career prospects. Meanwhile, Rachel visits work and finds out that during her leave, a guy named Gavin has been temporarily assigned to take care of her job. She starts to feel insecure about her job position and decides to come back from her leave early. Also, Phoebe works as an extra on Joey's TV show and brings even more drama to the set.
insight: Workplace reentry struggles yield middling returns despite consistent joke density across the hour.
